memoryArtifacts.headline: Anne Marbury Hutchinson
memoryArtifacts.description: Anne Marbury was born in Alford, Lincolnshire, England and christened there, in the Church of England 20 July 1591.  She was the third of fifteen children born to Reverend Francis Marbury and Bridget …

Origin Displayed: Scottish - Spelling variations of this family name include: Maule, Maull, Maul and others. First found in Yorkshire where they were seated from very early times and were granted lands by Duke William of Normandy, their liege Lord, for their distinguished assistance at the Battle of Hastings in 1066 C.E. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Christopher Maul settled in New York in 1709; John George Maul settled in Philadelphia in 1754; Thomas Maul settled in New England in 1617; Casper Maule settled in Philadelphia in 1753. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Motto Translated: Clemency concealed under rigour.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Mawle於1541年5月出生在英國素福爾可斯托克 白 納易藍德時，她的父親John Mawle Jr.29歲，母親Elizabeth Margaret Abbes20歲。她於1562年5月26日與John Barker在英國素福爾可納易藍德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和12個女兒的父母。她去世於1589年2月7日在英國素福爾可斯托克 白 納易藍德享年47歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可納易藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Mawle于1541年5月出生在英国素福爾可斯托克 白 納易藍德时，她的父亲John Mawle Jr.29岁，母亲Elizabeth Margaret Abbes20岁。她于1562年5月26日与John Barker在英国素福爾可納易藍德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和12个女儿的父母。她去世于1589年2月7日在英国素福爾可斯托克 白 納易藍德享年47岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可納易藍德。

variants.name: Mall
meanings: English: from the medieval female personal name Mall, a common form of Mald or Maud. See also Maude and Moll . It had several pet forms; see Malin , Malkin , and Mallet .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Mawle was born in May 1541, in Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England, her father, John Mawle Jr., was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth Margaret Abbes, was 20. She married John Barker on 26 May 1562, in Nayland, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 12 daughters. She died on 7 February 1589, in Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England, at the age of 47, and was buried in Nayland, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Mawle est née en mai 1541, à Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, John Mawle Jr., avait 29 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Margaret Abbes, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé John Barker le 26 mai 1562, à Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 12 filles. Elle est décédée le 7 février 1589, à Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterrée à Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Mawle im Mai 1541 in Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Mawle Jr. 29 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Margaret Abbes 20. Sie hat am 26. Mai 1562 in Nayland, Suffolk, England John Barker geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 12 Töchter. Sie starb am 7. Februar 1589 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England und wurde in Nayland, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Mawle è nata il nel maggio 1541 nel Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England, sua padre, John Mawle Jr., aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Margaret Abbes, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato John Barker il 26 maggio 1562, nel Nayland, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 12 figlie. Morì il 7 febbraio 1589, nel Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolta a Nayland, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Mawleが1541年5月にイギリススフォルクストク ビー ナイランドで生まれた時，父John Mawle Jr.は29歳，母Elizabeth Margaret Abbesは20歳であった。1562年5月26日にイギリススフォルクナイランドでJohn Barkerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と12人の娘の親であった。彼女は1589年2月7日にイギリススフォルクストク ビー ナイランドで47歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクナイランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Mawle(이)가 1541년 5월 영국 수폴크 스토크 비 나이랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Mawle Jr.(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Margaret Abbes(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1562년 5월 26일 영국 수폴크 나이랜드에서 John Barker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1589년 2월 7일 영국 수폴크 스토크 비 나이랜드에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 나이랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Mawle nasceu em maio de 1541, em Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Mawle Jr., tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Margaret Abbes, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com John Barker em 26 de maio de 1562, em Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 12 filhas. Ela faleceu em 7 de fevereiro de 1589, em Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 47 anos, e foi sepultada em Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Mawle родилась май 1541 г. в местности сток би неиланд, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени John Mawle Jr. было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Margaret Abbes, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Barker 26 мая 1562 г. в местности неиланд, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 12 дочерей. Она умерла 7 февраля 1589 г. в местности сток би неиланд, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 47 лет и была похоронена в местности неиланд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Mawle nació en mayo de 1541, en Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, John Mawle Jr., tenía 29 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Margaret Abbes, tenía 20 años. Se casó con John Barker el 26 de mayo de 1562, en Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 12 hijas. Murió el 7 de febrero de 1589, en Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultada en Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
